cards.term: The two leaders exchanged a polite, friendly greeting.
cards.definition: The two leaders exchanged a cordial greeting.

cards.term: She looked at the bribe with open contempt.
cards.definition: She looked at the bribe with disdain.

cards.term: not everything attractive is truly valuable (proverb)
cards.definition: All that glitters is not gold.

cards.term: She looked at the scoreboard and wondered if her eyes were playing tricks on her.
cards.definition: She looked at the scoreboard and whispered, "Do my eyes deceive me?"

cards.term: the feeling that someone or something is unworthy of respect; contempt; scorn
cards.definition: disdain

cards.term: so lacking in freshness or novelty as to be obvious, dull, and boring
cards.definition: banal

cards.term: The mask looked disturbingly warped in the lamplight.
cards.definition: The mask looked grotesque in the lamplight.

cards.term: His polite apology did not erase his history of lying to us.
cards.definition: His polite apology did not erase his duplicity.

cards.term: He said that once he won, his critics would be waiting on him.
cards.definition: He said, "All my haters become my waiters when I sit down at the table of success."

cards.term: The volunteers were too gung-ho about flagging every tiny paperwork error.
cards.definition: The volunteers were overzealous about flagging every tiny paperwork error.

cards.term: a really long time ago (informal, nostalgic)
cards.definition: many moons ago

cards.term: the state of having mixed feelings or contradictory ideas about something or someone
cards.definition: ambivalence

cards.term: have enough money to pay for
cards.definition: afford

cards.term: destroy utterly; obliterate
cards.definition: annihilate

cards.term: ready to accept control or instruction; submissive and easy to manage
cards.definition: docile

cards.term: When the volunteers clipped on the lead rope, the rescue horse offered no resistance.
cards.definition: When the volunteers clipped on the lead rope, the rescue horse was docile.

cards.term: The storm began to weaken after several hours of heavy rain.
cards.definition: The storm began to abate after several hours of heavy rain.

cards.term: when something is spot on; exactly right or perfectly timed (informal)
cards.definition: bullseye moment

cards.term: extreme greed for wealth or material gain
cards.definition: avarice

cards.term: The human brain is comparable to a computer.
cards.definition: The human brain is analogous to a computer.

cards.term: pleasant and agreeable in character; friendly and suited to one's company
cards.definition: congenial

cards.term: Their partnership started from a lucky chance meeting in an airport line.
cards.definition: Their partnership started from a serendipitous meeting in an airport line.

cards.term: The merch table glittered with hype, yet the album was unfinished, which showed that shine is not the same as substance.
cards.definition: The merch table glittered with hype, yet the album was unfinished—all that glitters is not gold.

cards.term: questioning whether you can trust what you see (idiom)
cards.definition: Do my eyes deceive me?

cards.term: Losing the deposit was the price of innocent poor judgment.
cards.definition: Losing the deposit was the price of naiveté.

cards.term: The new coach thanked the previous head coach.
cards.definition: The new coach thanked her predecessor.

cards.term: The company announced the purchase of a smaller firm.
cards.definition: The company announced the acquisition of a smaller firm.

cards.term: The team's performance was terrible in last night's game.
cards.definition: The team's performance was abysmal in last night's game.

cards.term: something that deviates from what is standard, normal, or expected
cards.definition: anomaly

cards.term: involving or characterized by conflict or opposition
cards.definition: adversarial

cards.term: The ending of the movie was unclear.
cards.definition: The ending of the movie was ambiguous.

cards.term: The arrival of smartphones has changed how we communicate.
cards.definition: The advent of smartphones has changed how we communicate.

cards.term: He hinted at his past without giving details.
cards.definition: He alluded to his past without giving details.

cards.term: the act of deceiving or misleading; behavior intended to make someone believe something false
cards.definition: deceit

cards.term: Wry joke about showing off modern tech or skill in the distant past
cards.definition: If you went back 1000 years and did this, you'd either start a religion or be burned alive.

cards.term: His love of money seemed to have no limit.
cards.definition: His avarice seemed to have no limit.

cards.term: to become less intense or widespread
cards.definition: abate

cards.term: skill in achieving one's ends by deceit or evasive cleverness
cards.definition: cunning

cards.term: satisfactory or acceptable in quality or quantity
cards.definition: adequate

cards.term: The old photograph was deeply moving in a quiet way.
cards.definition: The old photograph was poignant.

cards.term: Moving to a new city left him with mixed, conflicting feelings.
cards.definition: Moving to a new city left him with ambivalence.

cards.term: showy, confident, and striking in style or manner
cards.definition: flamboyant

cards.term: She is very driven and works hard.
cards.definition: She is very ambitious and works hard.

cards.term: innocent lack of experience, judgment, or sophistication; credulity (often in a gently critical sense)
cards.definition: naiveté

cards.term: stated or professed as true, but possibly not the real reason; outwardly appearing but possibly insincere
cards.definition: ostensible

cards.term: Independent bookstores are enjoying renewed excitement from young readers.
cards.definition: Independent bookstores are enjoying a renaissance of interest from young readers.

cards.term: The police finally caught the fugitive on the border.
cards.definition: The police finally apprehended the fugitive on the border.

cards.term: to make (something) suitable for a new use or purpose; modify
cards.definition: adapt

cards.term: The pianist's stage outfits were loud and hard to miss.
cards.definition: The pianist's stage outfits were flamboyant.

cards.term: having a great deal of money; wealthy
cards.definition: affluent

cards.term: We need to adjust our plan to fit the new schedule.
cards.definition: We need to adapt our plan to fit the new schedule.

cards.term: She is a supporter of environmental protection.
cards.definition: She is an advocate for environmental protection.

cards.term: The garden has a pleasing appearance.
cards.definition: The garden has a pleasing aesthetic.

cards.term: I can't pay for a new car right now.
cards.definition: I can't afford a new car right now.

cards.term: very bad
cards.definition: abysmal

cards.term: The bomb could destroy the entire city.
cards.definition: The bomb could annihilate the entire city.

cards.term: a person who held an office or post before the current holder; something that existed before and was replaced
cards.definition: predecessor

cards.term: a new era of; renewed interest in (formal or literary)
cards.definition: a renaissance of

cards.term: The critic's reviews were dense with bookish references and learning.
cards.definition: The critic's reviews were erudite.

cards.term: His reply was dead-on and named the problem nobody else dared to voice.
cards.definition: His reply was a bullseye moment and named the problem nobody else dared to voice.

cards.term: a person who publicly supports or recommends a particular cause or policy
cards.definition: advocate

cards.term: in a way that fits the facts; as a natural or logical result; therefore
cards.definition: accordingly

cards.term: The investigation was launched on his orders.
cards.definition: The investigation was launched at his behest.

cards.term: He was smooth, polite, and steady under pressure at the tense dinner.
cards.definition: He was urbane at the tense dinner.

cards.term: warm and friendly; sincere (especially of a welcome, manner, or relations)
cards.definition: cordial

cards.term: The relationship between the two companies was hostile.
cards.definition: The relationship between the two companies was adversarial.

cards.term: courteous, refined, and at ease; polished in manner (suggesting sophistication)
cards.definition: urbane

cards.term: having or showing great learning; deeply educated (often in literature or a narrow field)
cards.definition: erudite

cards.term: comparable in certain respects, typically in a way that makes clearer the nature of the things compared
cards.definition: analogous

cards.term: The test results showed something unusual.
cards.definition: The test results showed an anomaly.

cards.term: She studied hard and, as a result, did well on the exam.
cards.definition: She studied hard and, accordingly, did well on the exam.

cards.term: The two men had a noisy argument outside the store.
cards.definition: The two men had an altercation outside the store.

cards.term: concerned with beauty or the appreciation of beauty
cards.definition: aesthetic

cards.term: dealing with things sensibly and realistically; practical rather than idealistic
cards.definition: pragmatic

cards.term: evoking a keen sense of sadness, regret, or other deep, affecting feeling
cards.definition: poignant

cards.term: to arouse public concern and prompt action
cards.definition: to agitate

cards.term: His closing argument was a tired cliché everyone had heard before.
cards.definition: His closing argument was banal.

cards.term: Her answer was practical, blunt, and grounded in what the team could ship.
cards.definition: Her answer was pragmatic, blunt, and grounded in what the team could ship.

cards.term: The witness's alibi seemed unreliable on its face.
cards.definition: The witness's alibi seemed dubious on its face.

cards.term: The activists tried to stir public feeling in favor of stricter rent controls.
cards.definition: The activists tried to agitate for stricter rent controls.

cards.term: when you win, your critics serve you (brag)
cards.definition: All my haters become my waiters when I sit down at the table of success.

cards.term: They signed the waiver only after intense pressure from the manager.
cards.definition: They signed the waiver under coercion from the manager.

cards.term: open to more than one interpretation; not having one obvious meaning
cards.definition: ambiguous

cards.term: She learned to drive a very long time ago.
cards.definition: Many moons ago, she learned to drive.

cards.term: a noisy argument or disagreement, especially in public
cards.definition: altercation

cards.term: the process of gaining possession of something
cards.definition: acquisition

cards.term: betrayal of trust; treacherous action
cards.definition: treachery

cards.term: let (someone) have or do something
cards.definition: allow

cards.term: The teacher will give extra time for the test.
cards.definition: The teacher will allow extra time for the test.

cards.term: hesitating to trust; of doubtful value, veracity, or quality; suspect
cards.definition: dubious

cards.term: The other people at the table were warm and easy to talk to.
cards.definition: The other people at the table were congenial.

cards.term: to arrest (someone, especially a criminal suspect) for a crime; to take into custody
cards.definition: to apprehend

cards.term: They lived in a wealthy neighborhood with large houses and private schools.
cards.definition: They lived in an affluent neighborhood with large houses and private schools.

cards.term: The supply of food was sufficient for the camping trip.
cards.definition: The supply of food was adequate for the camping trip.

cards.term: The thief relied on clever tricks rather than on force.
cards.definition: The thief relied on cunning rather than on force.

cards.term: He is skilled at solving complex math problems.
cards.definition: He is adept at solving complex math problems.

cards.term: Most people have no idea how to do stuff like this
cards.definition: If you went back 1000 years and did this, you'd either start a religion or be burned alive.

cards.term: Her paintings were non-representational, built from shapes and colors.
cards.definition: Her paintings were abstract, built from shapes and colors.

cards.term: occurring or discovered by a fortunate accident; opportune and unplanned
cards.definition: serendipitous

cards.term: His friendly manner masked outright dishonesty.
cards.definition: His friendly manner masked outright deceit.

cards.term: having or showing a strong desire and determination to succeed
cards.definition: ambitious

cards.term: showing excessive enthusiasm or fervor
cards.definition: overzealous

cards.term: existing in thought or as an idea but not having a physical or concrete existence
cards.definition: abstract

cards.term: Among the interns, she was the clearest example of professionalism.
cards.definition: Among the interns, she was a paragon of professionalism.

cards.term: having skill in a particular area
cards.definition: adept

cards.term: the use of force or threats to make someone act against their will; compulsion
cards.definition: coercion

cards.term: deceitfulness in speech or conduct; double-dealing
cards.definition: duplicity

cards.term: suggest or call attention to indirectly; hint at
cards.definition: allude

cards.term: according to what he wanted
cards.definition: at his behest

cards.term: (of a victory) won at such a cost that it is hardly worth winning
cards.definition: pyrrhic

cards.term: The king saw the secret alliance as a stab in the back.
cards.definition: The king saw the secret alliance as treachery.

cards.term: the arrival of a notable person or thing
cards.definition: advent

cards.term: a person or thing regarded as a perfect example of a quality
cards.definition: paragon

cards.term: The settlement looked like a win on paper, but the fees had swallowed the upside.
cards.definition: The settlement was a pyrrhic victory—the fees had swallowed the upside.

cards.term: comically or repulsively ugly, distorted, or wrong (often shockingly so)
cards.definition: grotesque

cards.term: The official reason for the trip was a client meeting.
cards.definition: The ostensible reason for the trip was a client meeting.
